cards.title: 🟡 Computed dependency tracking is top-level only
cards.desc:
The Proxy in #getComputedDependencies records only the first property accessed. (s) => s.user.name tracks "user", so any change to user — even unrelated nested keys — triggers recomputation.

Fix: Track full dot-separated paths recursively through the Proxy using a path-accumulating handler.

cards.title: 🟡 #querySelectorAllDeep misses closed flowThrough shadows
cards.desc:
Deep queries only recurse into el.shadowRoot (open shadows). Declarative bindings inside closed shadow roots registered via through() on nested components are never found.

Fix: After scanning open shadows, also iterate this.#flowThroughs and recurse into each registered ShadowRoot.

cards.title: 🟡 #hasOverwritingFlow blind to closed shadows
cards.desc:
The overwrite check reads current.shadowRoot, which is null for closed shadows. A nested closed-shadow FlowState scope won't block an ancestor from stomping its bindings.

Fix: Store closed ShadowRoot refs via through() and check them during the upward traversal.

cards.title: 🟡 #update is async but never awaited
cards.desc:
#update is declared async but contains no await expressions. It returns a Promise nobody handles — misleading and could mask future async bugs.

Fix: Remove the async keyword. The method is synchronous.

cards.title: 🟠 No disconnectedCallback / cleanup
cards.desc:
Watcher entries, registry entries, and __Flow__ stay attached for the page lifetime even when root elements are removed. In SPAs with frequent mount/unmount this causes unbounded growth.

Fix: Add disconnectedCallback (or destroy()) to remove the registry entry, clear watchers, and delete __Flow__.

cards.title: 🟠 flowThroughs holds strong ShadowRoot references
cards.desc:
The #flowThroughs Map keeps a strong ref to every registered ShadowRoot. Removing a component from the DOM does not release its shadow root from memory.

Fix: Use WeakRef for Map values, or clean up entries in disconnectedCallback.

cards.title: 🔵 through() has no guard against pre-init calls
cards.desc:
FlowState.through() dispatches an event that must be caught by a FlowState listener on the host. Called before that instance exists, the event fires with no listener and is silently lost.

Fix: Document the ordering requirement clearly, or queue the linkage and replay it when a FlowState instance later mounts on the host.

cards.title: 🔵 Prototype-collision state key names
cards.desc:
#setNested and #mergeValues iterate keys without filtering reserved names. A key named __proto__, constructor, or toString could corrupt the object prototype.

Fix: Skip or throw for keys that collide with Object.prototype property names (use Object.hasOwn guard).

cards.title: 🔴 Batched functional updates share same snapshot
cards.desc:
Multiple state.update(s => ...) calls in one microtask all receive structuredClone(this.#values) — the pre-flush state. The second function never sees the first's result.

Fix: Accumulate a running draft and pass it to each successive functional update before merging into state.

cards.title: 🔴 #get skips falsy hook values
cards.desc:
if (this.#hooks[key]) is falsy for hooks whose value is 0, false, null, or "". Those hooks are silently ignored and undefined is returned instead.

Fix: Change to if (key in this.#hooks) — the same pattern already used correctly in #watch.

cards.title: 🔴 Duplicate-mount guard returns wrong type
cards.desc:
When a second FlowState is mounted on the same root the early-return gives callers { hasKey, flowThroughs } instead of { update, watch, get, through }.

Fix: Throw an error, or stash the real instanceApi on the root and return it from the guard.

cards.title: 🔴 #sourceElRegistry leaks — WeakRef values but the Map key is a string ID
cards.desc: #sourceElRegistry is a Map<string, WeakRef> — the WeakRef lets the element be collected but the string → WeakRef entry in the Map itself is never cleaned up. Every kanban-card that ever connected will accumulate an entry forever. Needs a FinalizationRegistry to prune stale entries, or a different approach.
